codeReview.summary: The fix correctly addresses the non-deterministic ordering of OAuth status cards by adding sort.Slice to the OAuthStatuses() method, sorting alphabetically by BackendName. The implementation is minimal, correct, and well-tested. The worker followed a test-first approach (wrote the failing test, then applied the fix), ran gofmt, and verified with -count=10 to stress-test determinism.

summary: Clean, minimal fix. The OAuthStatuses() method in registry.go now sorts results by BackendName using sort.Slice, ensuring deterministic alphabetical ordering on the settings page. Three new tests cover the multi-backend ordering case (with intentionally misordered names to expose map randomness), single backend, and empty registry edge cases. All tests pass reliably with -count=10. The template (oauth_status.html) already iterates in the order provided, so no template changes were needed. The fix correctly identified the root cause (map iteration is non-deterministic in Go) and applied the simplest possible solution.
